sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Parma are struggling, having failed to win in their last 3 matches with no goals scored during this period. Juventus, on the other hand, are in solid form, unbeaten in their last 3 games, including a strong 3-0 win over Napoli and clean sheets in their last 3 matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Juventus have dominated with a 2-0 victory in August 2025, though Parma managed a 1-0 upset in April 2025. Historically, Juventus hold the edge with 3 wins in the last 5 matchups against Parma.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Juventus, sitting 5th with 42 points, have a potent attack (35 goals scored) and a solid defense (17 conceded). Parma, languishing in 16th with 23 points, have struggled offensively with just 14 goals, one of the lowest in the league, while conceding 26.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Juventus, Kenan Yıldız (8 goals, 4 assists) and Manuel Locatelli (high Sofascore ratings) are in excellent form. Parma will rely on Mateo Pellegrino (6 goals) to break their scoring drought, though their overall attacking output remains limited.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Juventus are likely to control possession (averaging higher ball possession) and exploit Parma’s defensive frailties. Parma may focus on counter-attacks, given their low conversion rate (28.2% of big chances) and Juventus’ recent defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Juventus’ superior form, attacking strength, and defensive stability, coupled with Parma’s inability to score recently, the odds favor an away win. Sofascore’s prediction of a 51% chance for Juventus aligns with current trends, though a draw (31%) remains possible if Parma defend tightly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Ennio Tardini saw Juventus take a commanding lead over Parma with a 2-0 scoreline. Juventus dominated much of the game, showing clinical finishing with goals from Bremer and Weston McKennie. Parma struggled to create significant scoring opportunities and failed to convert their limited chances into goals. The away side's control over possession and ability to penetrate Parma's defense were evident throughout the half. Parma, on the other hand, showed resilience in defense at times but lacked the cutting edge in attack.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Juventus displayed a superior performance in the first half, controlling the majority of the ball and creating more dangerous chances. Their ability to enter the final third and penetrate the penalty area was notably better than Parma's, which reflects in their two goals scored. Parma, despite having a fair share of corner kicks, could not capitalize on set pieces or open play. Juventus also showed strength in aerial duels, giving them an edge in physical battles. Parma's statistics reveal a team struggling to match the intensity and quality of their opponents, with limited entries into dangerous areas.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: For Juventus, Weston McKennie stood out with an impressive contribution, scoring a goal and consistently threatening Parma's defense. Bremer also made his mark by heading in the opener and maintaining a solid defensive presence. On the Parma side, players like Mandela Keita showed promise with strong defensive efforts, but the attacking players, including Gaetano Oristanio and Emanuele Valeri, couldn't create the necessary impact in the final third. The lack of a creative spark in Parma's lineup was evident, while Juventus had multiple players stepping up in key moments.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half was heavily tilted towards Juventus for the majority of the time, with consistent attacking spells particularly in the early stages and around the moments of their goals. Parma had brief periods where they pushed forward, especially in the middle of the half and towards the end of the first 45 minutes, showing some intent to get back into the game. However, Juventus quickly regained control after these short bursts from the home side, maintaining pressure and not allowing Parma to build sustained attacks. The shifts in momentum underline Juventus' dominance in dictating the pace of the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Juventus are likely to continue their aggressive approach, leveraging their lead to control the game and potentially add to their tally. Parma will need to come out with renewed energy and a more effective attacking strategy if they hope to claw back into the match. The home side might focus on exploiting set pieces or counter-attacks, as Juventus could sit back slightly to protect their lead. Disciplinary issues could also play a role, as Juventus have already picked up a few yellow cards, which might impact their approach. Overall, the second half could see at least one more goal as spaces open up, especially if Parma push for a comeback.
